goal: Implement P1.1: make the orchestrate loop's pause button actually halt execution. The pause_requested flag exists on OrchestrateRun and the UI endpoint flips it, but the executor loop never checks it — users click Pause yet the run keeps consuming tasks and budget.

tasks.name: scaffold-platform-workspace
tasks.description: Create the project directory structure and install dependencies. This task IS the scaffolding (package, deps, health endpoint, alembic, conftest). NOT the OrchestrateRun model or any feature logic.
tasks.instruction: Create platform/ package: platform/app/__init__.py (empty), platform/app/main.py (FastAPI app with GET /health -> 200 {status: ok}), platform/requirements.txt (fastapi, uvicorn[standard], sqlalchemy, alembic, psycopg2-binary, pytest, httpx). Create platform/app/database.py: engine from DATABASE_URL env var, TestingSessionLocal from TEST_DATABASE_URL, get_db() dependency yielding a session. Create alembic.ini and platform/alembic/env.py wired to DATABASE_URL. Create platform/tests/__init__.py and platform/tests/conftest.py (db session fixture using TEST_DATABASE_URL that overrides get_db). Verify: cd platform && pytest --collect-only 2>&1 must show no import errors.

acceptance_criteria.text: pytest --collect-only succeeds without import errors on platform/tests/
acceptance_criteria.check: Run: cd platform && pytest --collect-only; expect 0 errors and at least conftest.py listed in collected items

exclusions: Do NOT create any endpoint beyond /health

produces.workspace: platform/ FastAPI+Alembic+pytest scaffold with database.py providing get_db()

tasks.name: add-orchestrate-run-model
tasks.description: Define OrchestrateRun SQLAlchemy model with pause fields and run the Alembic migration. This task IS the model + migration. NOT the executor or API endpoints.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/models.py create OrchestrateRun with columns: id (UUID PK, default uuid4), status (String(20), default RUNNING), pause_requested (Boolean, default False, nullable=False), paused_at (DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True), created_at (DateTime, default utcnow), updated_at (DateTime, default utcnow, onupdate utcnow). Use Base from platform/app/database.py (from _1 produces). Generate alembic migration: alembic revision --autogenerate -m add_orchestrate_run. Run: alembic upgrade head. Write test platform/tests/test_p1_pause.py::test_orchestrate_run_model_pause_fields: create a row with pause_requested=False, flush, query back, assert pause_requested==False and paused_at is None. Do NOT create executor.py or routers/.

acceptance_criteria.text: OrchestrateRun row can be created with pause_requested=False and queried back

exclusions: Do NOT modify main.py beyond Base metadata import

produces.model: OrchestrateRun(id:UUID, status:str=RUNNING, pause_requested:bool=False, paused_at:datetime|null) in platform/app/models.py

tasks.name: add-check-pause-executor-gate
tasks.description: Implement _check_pause() cooperative gate and run_orchestration() loop in platform/app/executor.py. This task IS the executor logic that makes pause work. NOT the API endpoints.
tasks.instruction: Create platform/app/executor.py. Implement _check_pause(db: Session, run_id: UUID) -> bool: re-fetches OrchestrateRun from DB (db.query(OrchestrateRun).filter_by(id=run_id).first()), returns True if pause_requested==True else False. Implement run_orchestration(run_id: UUID, db: Session): simulated 3-iteration loop, on each iteration call _check_pause(db, run_id) - if True: re-fetch run, set run.status='PAUSED', run.paused_at=datetime.utcnow(), db.commit(), return. OrchestrateRun model is in platform/app/models.py (from _2 produces). Add tests in platform/tests/test_p1_pause.py: test_check_pause_helper_detects_flag (set pause_requested=True in DB, call _check_pause, assert True); test_run_orchestration_halts_on_pause (create run with pause_requested=True, call run_orchestration, assert run.status==PAUSED and paused_at is not None). Do NOT touch routers/ or main.py.

acceptance_criteria.text: _check_pause returns True when pause_requested=True in DB (fresh read)
run_orchestration sets status=PAUSED and paused_at when pause flag is set

exclusions: Do NOT create or modify routers/

tasks.name: add-pause-endpoint
tasks.description: Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/pause endpoint. This task IS the pause trigger API. NOT resume, NOT status, NOT executor.
tasks.instruction: Create platform/app/routers/__init__.py (empty) and platform/app/routers/runs.py with APIRouter. Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{run_id}/pause: fetch OrchestrateRun by run_id, return 404 if not found, set pause_requested=True, db.commit(), return 200 {id: str(run.id), pause_requested: true}. Wire router into platform/app/main.py: app.include_router(runs.router). OrchestrateRun model from platform/app/models.py (from _2 produces). get_db from platform/app/database.py (from _1 produces). Add test platform/tests/test_p1_pause.py::test_pause_endpoint_sets_flag: POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/pause via httpx TestClient, assert 200 and re-query DB to confirm pause_requested=True. Do NOT touch executor.py. Do NOT implement resume or status endpoints here.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/pause returns 200 and sets pause_requested=True in DB

tasks.name: add-resume-endpoint
tasks.description: Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume endpoint. This task IS the resume API. NOT pause, NOT status.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/routers/runs.py (from _4 produces: file exists with router wired to main.py) add POST /orchestrate-runs/{run_id}/resume: fetch OrchestrateRun, return 404 if not found, set pause_requested=False, set status='RUNNING', db.commit(), return 200 {id: str(run.id), status: RUNNING, pause_requested: false}. Add test platform/tests/test_p1_pause.py::test_resume_endpoint_clears_flag: create run with pause_requested=True status=PAUSED, POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume via TestClient, assert 200 and DB has pause_requested=False and status=RUNNING. Do NOT modify pause endpoint. Do NOT add status endpoint here.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume returns 200, sets pause_requested=False and status=RUNNING in DB

exclusions: Do NOT add status endpoint here

tasks.name: expose-pause-fields-in-status-endpoint
tasks.description: Add GET /orchestrate-runs/{id}/status endpoint exposing pause_requested and paused_at. This task IS the observability endpoint. NOT the pause/resume logic.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/routers/runs.py (from _5: file exists with pause+resume endpoints and router wired) add GET /orchestrate-runs/{run_id}/status: fetch OrchestrateRun, return 404 if not found, return 200 {id: str(run.id), status: run.status, pause_requested: run.pause_requested, paused_at: run.paused_at.isoformat() if run.paused_at else None}. Add test platform/tests/test_p1_pause.py::test_status_endpoint_exposes_pause_fields: create run with pause_requested=True paused_at=datetime.utcnow(), GET /orchestrate-runs/{id}/status via TestClient, assert 200 and pause_requested==True and paused_at is not null in response. Do NOT modify pause or resume endpoints. Do NOT modify executor.py.

acceptance_criteria.text: GET /orchestrate-runs/{id}/status returns 200 with pause_requested=True and non-null paused_at when run has been paused

exclusions: Do NOT modify pause or resume endpoints
